Snooker Exam

Assessment: Unknown — —

Shot List

    Basic

    Straight pot

    Roll the red in to get postion on the black.

    Long pot and hold for black

    Either roll the red in or hit a long dragging stun to hold for the black

    Finesse positional play

    Hit slowly with a hair of running side to land nicely on the black

    Black to yellow positional

    Slow pace with LHS to land nicely on the yellow

    Yellow to green positional

    Slow pace to land nicely on the green, slower than you think.

    Pinching the pocket

    Pot the red into the right of the pocket to generate angle to stun down for nice position on the black

    Red ball plant

    Make the plant and return for position on baulk colour or blue

    Advanced

    The break off

    Low speed lots of side and aim to swing the ball around the angles and finish in the baulk area.

    Soft stun long blue

    Hit low to retain just enough stun when potting the blue, this kills the pace of the cue ball to hold nicely on the red

    Screw back for position

    Screw back off the red to get good position on the blue.

    Laying a snooker

    Stun the yellow down the table, hold the white behind the brown and the yellow should finish near the black making the saftey hard to escape

    Open the pack off black

    Pot the black along the cushion with pace to break open the pack of reds
